explanatory_strength.rationale: Competitive-advantage mechanics remain well specified: AWS re-acceleration (+28% Q1-26, 37.7% segment margin), Prime and logistics density, ads attach, and custom silicon lowering AWS cost of goods sold. Structural versus cyclical labeling is explicit, and the primary inversion (permanent AI capex overshoot) is directly tested against TTM operating cash flow $148.5B versus purchases ~$151B. The research loop did not weaken the moat narrative; it confirmed the capital-conversion path is still unproven.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Round-two research confirms the contract owner_earnings input ($7.695B) is irreconcilable with TTM operating cash flow $148.5B, so the reinvestment DCF proof cannot price the security. The filing- or management-grounded bridge from ~$151B spot capex to ~$90B sustainable mid-cycle spend remains explicitly unresolved; management_facts has zero documents and filing_facts_2026-07-20.json is a stale 2003 extract. Incremental after-tax ROIC bounds (12-25%) are judgment without a primary capital ledger.

downside_control.rationale: Operating cash flow scale ($148.5B TTM), cash ($86.8B), and equity ($411.1B) bound liquidity stress, and capex-stress free cash flow of ~$0.11/sh is quantified. Downside is not tight: multiple compression on a franchise priced for AI optionality and a prolonged hyperscaler capex arms race could leave equity returns in low single digits even if operating cash flow stays large.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Using the Lawrence normalized bridge (~$5.35/sh owner cash), total synthesis is 3.68% per year at ~$247, Lawrence gate 1.9%, and segment sum-implied 4.1%; all sit well below a ~15% medium-term hurdle. Bull sensitivity to 7.2% (scenario) or 16.2% (assumption-heavy $9/sh normalized owner cash) does not clear the bar on filing-grounded base economics, and the broken contract proof cannot substitute.

claims.claim: Targeted research confirmed valuation_contract owner_earnings of $7.695B does not match TTM operating cash flow $148.5B ($148,531M); committee pricing must use the Lawrence normalized bridge (~$58.5B / ~$5.35 per share), not the contract reinvestment DCF row.
Q1 2026 AWS net sales were $37.6 billion (+28% YoY) with segment operating income margin 37.7%, supporting durable cloud competitive advantage independent of capex normalization timing.

The ~$90 billion sustainable capex assumption and observable incremental return on AI datacenter capital remain open: research loop status unresolved with no management session or filing disclosure on normalization timing.

strongest_counter_explanation: Amazon is rationally priced as a perpetual AI infrastructure compounder: hyperscaler capex competition keeps capital purchases near operating cash flow for years, so reported AWS and ads growth never converts to normalized owner cash per share at a rate that clears a 15% equity return at today's multiple.
most_important_missing_fact: Management-stated sustainable data-center capex run rate, incremental after-tax return on AI and datacenter capital from a filing-grounded capital ledger, and a disclosed timeline for when build-out spending moderates enough to lift normalized free cash flow per share toward the $5.35-$9.00 per share bridge.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with operating cash flow growth below 5% YoY while capital purchases stay above $140 billion
AWS revenue growth decelerates below 15% while segment operating margin compresses versus Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud
Normalized owner cash per share fails to inflect above $4 by FY2028 despite capex moderating toward the assumed ~$90 billion run rate
specialist_findings: Competitive-advantage overlap controls remain sound (advertising embedded, not double-counted; AI backlog embedded_in_segment). Economic claim audit: moat pillars filing-anchored; capital-requirement audit fails decision grade because spot capex ~$151B versus assumed ~$90B mid-cycle lacks management or SEC bridge. Valuation-proof audit: contract calculation_proof arithmetic is internally valid but starts from a factually wrong owner_earnings input; Lawrence bridge is the only usable owner-cash path. Comparable/segment build implies 4.1% at explicit discount, consistent with sub-hurdle consolidated returns. Option probabilities (ads, agentic AI ROI) remain judgment with zero terminal in option scan. No management fieldwork this period.
